documentation:
# Disease Normalizer

[![image](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/disease-normalizer.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/disease-normalizer)
[![image](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/disease-normalizer.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/disease-normalizer)
[![image](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/disease-normalizer.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/disease-normalizer)
[![Actions status](https://github.com/cancervariants/disease-normalization/actions/workflows/checks.yaml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/cancervariants/disease-normalization/actions/workflows/checks.yaml)

<!-- description -->
The Disease Normalizer resolves ambiguous references and descriptions of human diseases to consistently-structured, normalized terms. For concepts extracted from [NCIt](https://ncithesaurus.nci.nih.gov/ncitbrowser/), [Mondo Disease Ontology](https://mondo.monarchinitiative.org/), [The Human Disease Ontology](https://disease-ontology.org/), [OMIM](https://www.omim.org/), and [OncoTree](https://oncotree.info/#/home), it designates a [CURIE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/CURIE), and provides additional metadata like aliases and cross-references.
<!-- /description -->

---

**[Documentation](https://disease-normalizer.readthedocs.io/latest/)** · [Installation](https://disease-normalizer.readthedocs.io/latest/install.html) · [API reference](https://disease-normalizer.readthedocs.io/latest/reference/index.html)

---

## Installation

The Disease Normalizer is available via PyPI:

```commandline

python3 -m pip install disease-normalizer
```

See [installation instructions](https://disease-normalizer.readthedocs.io/latest/install.html) in the documentation for a description of installation options and data setup requirements.

---

## Examples

Use the [live service](https://normalize.cancervariants.org/disease/) to programmatically normalize disease terms, as in the following truncated example:

```shell
$ curl -s 'https://normalize.cancervariants.org/disease/normalize?q=liver%20cancer' | python -m json.tool
{
    "query": "liver cancer",
    "warnings": null,
    "match_type": 80,
    "normalized_id": "ncit:C34803",
    "disease": {
        "type": "Disease",
        "id": "normalize.disease:liver%20cancer",
        "label": "Primary Malignant Liver Neoplasm",
        # ...
    }
}
```

Or utilize the [Python API](https://disease-normalizer.readthedocs.io/latest/reference/api/disease.query.html) for fast local access:

```pycon
>>> from disease.query import QueryHandler
>>> from disease.database import create_db
>>> q = QueryHandler(create_db())
>>> result = q.normalize("NSCLC")
>>> result.normalized_id
'ncit:C2926'
```

---

## Feedback and contributing

We welcome bug reports, feature requests, and code contributions from users and interested collaborators. The [documentation](https://disease-normalizer.readthedocs.io/latest/contributing.html) contains guidance for submitting feedback and contributing new code.

modules.name: disease.etl.merge
documentation: Create concept groups and merged records.

classes.name: disease.etl.merge.Merge

methods.name: disease.etl.merge.Merge.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Merge instance.

```
param Database database

db instance to use for record retrieval and creation.

param silent

if True, suppress console output
```

methods.name: disease.etl.merge.Merge.create_merged_concepts
documentation:
Create concept groups, generate merged concept records, and update database.

Our normalization protocols only generate record ID sets that include Mondo
terms, meaning only Mondo IDs should be submitted to this method.

```
param record_ids

concept identifiers from which groups should be generated.
```

methods.name: disease.etl.merge.Merge._generate_merged_record
documentation:
Generate merged record from provided concept ID group.
Where attributes are sets, they should be merged, and where they are
scalars, assign from the highest-priority source where that attribute
is non-null.

Priority is NCIt > Mondo > OMIM > OncoTree> DO.

```
param Set record_id_set

group of concept IDs

return

completed merged drug object to be stored in DB, as well as
a list of the IDs ultimately included in said record
```
Manage construction of record mergers for normalization.

documentation:
Get dbXref from xref clause.

In the Mondo OBO distribution, some xrefs only show up in explicit xref clauses.
This method processes them.

```
param clause

xref clause from term frame

return

prefix and local ID if available
```

methods.name: disease.etl.mondo.Mondo._get_xref_from_pv_clause
documentation:
Get dbXref from property value clause.

These are a bit more semantically rich than the Mondo xref clauses, so for now,
we prefer to fetch most references from here.

```
param clause

property value clause

return

prefix and local ID if available
```

modules.name: disease.database.database
documentation:
Provide core database classes and helper methods.

Users shouldn't need to interact with the base class directly, but
{py:meth}`~disease.database.database.create_db` is the recommended way to create a
database connection.

functions.name: disease.database.database.confirm_aws_db_use
documentation: Check to ensure that AWS instance should actually be used.

functions.name: disease.database.database.create_db
documentation:
Database factory method. Checks environment variables and provided parameters
and creates a DB instance.

Generally prefers to return a DynamoDB instance, unless all DDB-relevant
environment variables are unset and a libpq-compliant URI is passed to
`db_url`.

```
param db_url

address to database instance

param aws_instance

use hosted DynamoDB instance, not local DB

return

constructed Database instance
```

classes.name: disease.database.database.DatabaseException
documentation: Create custom class for handling database exceptions

classes.name: disease.database.database.DatabaseInitializationException
documentation: Create custom exception for errors during DB connection initialization.

classes.name: disease.database.database.DatabaseReadException
documentation: Create custom exception for lookup/read errors

classes.name: disease.database.database.DatabaseWriteException
documentation: Create custom exception for write errors

classes.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.__init__
documentation:
Initialize database instance.

Generally, implementing classes should be able to construct a connection by
something like a libpq URL. Any additional arguments or DB-specific parameters
can be passed as keywords.

```
param db_url

address/connection description for database

param db_args

any DB implementation-specific parameters

raise DatabaseInitializationException

if initial setup fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.list_tables
documentation:
Return names of tables in database.

```
return

Table names in database
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase._check_delete_okay
documentation:
Check that environmental conditions permit DB deletion, and require
confirmation.

:raise DatabaseWriteException: if skip confirmation variable is set -- manual
approval is required.

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.drop_db
documentation:
Initiate total teardown of DB. Useful for quickly resetting the entirety of
the data. Requires manual confirmation.

:raise DatabaseWriteException: if called in a protected setting with
    confirmation silenced.

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.check_schema_initialized
documentation:
Check if database schema is properly initialized.

```
return

True if DB appears to be fully initialized, False otherwise
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.check_tables_populated
documentation:
Perform rudimentary checks to see if tables are populated.
Emphasis is on rudimentary -- if some fiendish element has deleted half of the
disease aliases, this method won't pick it up. It just wants to see if a few
critical tables have at least a small number of records.

```
return

True if queries successful, false if DB appears empty
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.initialize_db
documentation:
Perform all necessary parts of database setup. Should be tolerant of
existing content -- ie, this method is also responsible for checking whether
the DB is already set up.

:raise DatabaseInitializationException: if initialization fails

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.get_source_metadata
documentation:
Get license, versioning, data lookup, etc information for a source.

```
param src_name

name of the source to get data for

return

source metadata, if lookup is successful
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.get_record_by_id
documentation:
Fetch record corresponding to provided concept ID

```
param concept_id

concept ID for record

param case_sensitive

if true, performs exact lookup, which may be quicker.
Otherwise, performs filter operation, which doesn't require correct casing.

param merge

if true, look for merged record; look for identity
record otherwise.

return

complete record, if match is found; None otherwise
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.get_refs_by_type
documentation:
Retrieve concept IDs for records matching the user's query. Other methods
are responsible for actually retrieving full records.

```
param search_term

string to match against

param ref_type

type of match to look for.

return

list of associated concept IDs. Empty if lookup fails.
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.get_all_concept_ids
documentation:
Retrieve all available concept IDs for use in generating normalized records.

```
param source

optionally, just get all IDs for a specific source

return

Set of concept IDs as strings.
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.get_all_records
documentation:
Retrieve all source or normalized records. Either return all source records,
or all records that qualify as "normalized" (i.e., merged groups + source
records that are otherwise ungrouped).
For example,

```pycon
>>> from disease.database import create_db
>>> from disease.schemas import RecordType
>>> db = create_db()
>>> for record in db.get_all_records(RecordType.MERGER):
>>>     pass  # do something
```

```
param record_type

type of result to return

return

Generator that lazily provides records as they are retrieved
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.add_source_metadata
documentation:
Add new source metadata entry.

```
param src_name

name of source

param meta

known source attributes

raise DatabaseWriteException

if write fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.add_record
documentation:
Add new record to database.

```
param record

record to upload

param src_name

name of source for record.
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.add_merged_record
documentation:
Add merged record to database.

```
param record

merged record to add
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.update_merge_ref
documentation:
Update the merged record reference of an individual record to a new value.

```
param concept_id

record to update

param merge_ref

new ref value

raise DatabaseWriteException

if attempting to update non-existent record
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.delete_normalized_concepts
documentation:
Remove merged records from the database. Use when performing a new update
of normalized data.

:raise DatabaseReadException: if DB client requires separate read calls and
    encounters a failure in the process
:raise DatabaseWriteException: if deletion call fails

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.delete_source
documentation:
Delete all data for a source. Use when updating source data.

```
param src_name

name of source to delete

raise DatabaseReadException

if DB client requires separate read calls and
encounters a failure in the process

raise DatabaseWriteException

if deletion call fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.complete_write_transaction
documentation: Conclude transaction or batch writing if relevant.

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.close_connection
documentation: Perform any manual connection closure procedures if necessary.

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.load_from_remote
documentation:
Load DB from remote dump. Warning: Deletes all existing data.

```
param url

remote location to retrieve gzipped dump file from

raise

NotImplementedError if not supported by DB
```

methods.name: disease.database.database.AbstractDatabase.export_db
documentation:
Dump DB to specified location.

```
param export_location

path to save DB dump at

raise

NotImplementedError if not supported by DB
```

modules.name: disease
documentation: The VICC library for normalizing diseases.

modules.name: disease.schemas
documentation: Contains data models for representing VICC normalized disease records.

classes.name: disease.schemas.MatchType
documentation: Define string constraints for use in Match Type attributes.

classes.name: disease.schemas.SourceName
documentation: Define string constraints to ensure consistent capitalization.

documentation: noqa: PIE796

class_variables.name: disease.schemas.SourceIDAfterNamespace.ONCOTREE
documentation: noqa: PIE796

documentation: noqa: PIE796
Define string constraints after namespace.

documentation:
Define string constraints for how concept ID namespace prefixes are
stored.

classes.name: disease.schemas.SourcePriority
documentation: Define priorities for sources in building merged concepts.

documentation: Define disease record.

documentation: Record item types.

documentation: Reference item types.

documentation: Define constraints for data license attributes.

documentation: Metadata for a given source to return in response object.

documentation: Metadata regarding the disease-normalization service.

classes.name: disease.schemas.NormalizationService
documentation: Response containing one or more merged records and source data.

classes.name: disease.schemas.SearchService
documentation: Core response schema containing matches for each source

modules.name: disease.query
documentation: Provides query handler class, which receives and responses to user search queries.

classes.name: disease.query.InvalidParameterException
documentation: Exception for invalid parameter args provided by the user.

classes.name: disease.query.QueryHandler

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler.__init__
documentation:
Initialize QueryHandler instance. Requires a created database object to
initialize. The most straightforward way to do this is via the
{py:meth}`~disease.database.database.create_db` method:

```pycon
>>> from disease.query import QueryHandler
>>> from disease.database import create_db
>>> q = QueryHandler(create_db())
```

We'll generally call `create_db` without any arguments in code examples, for
the sake of brevity. See the {py:meth}`~disease.database.database.create_db` API
description for more details.

```
param database

storage backend to search against
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._emit_warnings
documentation:
Emit warnings if query contains non breaking space characters.
\:param str query_str: query string
\:return: dict keying warning type to warning description

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._add_record
documentation:
Add individual record to response object

```
param Dict[str, Dict] response

in-progress response object to return
to client

param Dict item

Item retrieved from DynamoDB

param str match_type

type of query match

return

Tuple containing updated response object, and string
containing name of the source of the match
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._fetch_records
documentation:
Return matched Disease records as a structured response for a given
collection of concept IDs.

```
param Dict[str, Dict] response

in-progress response object to return
to client.

param List[str] concept_ids

List of concept IDs to build from.
Should be all lower-case.

param str match_type

record should be assigned this type of match.

return

response Dict with records filled in via provided concept
IDs, and Set of source names of matched records
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._fill_no_matches
documentation:
Fill all empty source_matches slots with NO_MATCH results.

```
param Dict[str, Dict] resp

incoming response object

return

response object with empty source slots filled with
NO_MATCH results and corresponding source metadata
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._check_concept_id
documentation:
Check query for concept ID match. Should only find 0 or 1 matches.

```
param str query

search string

param Dict resp

in-progress response object to return to client

param Set[str] sources

remaining unmatched sources

return

Tuple with updated resp object and updated set of unmatched
sources
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._check_match_type
documentation:
Check query for selected match type.

```
param str query

search string

param Dict resp

in-progress response object to return to client

param Set[str] sources

remaining unmatched sources

param RefType match_type

Match type to check for. Should be one of
{'label', 'alias', 'xref', 'associated_with'}

return

Tuple with updated resp object and updated set of unmatched
sources
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._get_search_response
documentation:
Return response as dict where key is source name and value is a list of
records.

```
param str query

string to match against

param Set[str] sources

sources to match from

return

completed response object to return to client
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler.search
documentation:
Return highest match for each source.

```pycon
>>> from disease.query import QueryHandler
>>> from disease.database import create_db
>>> q = QueryHandler(create_db())
>>> result = q.search("NSCLC")
>>> result.source_matches["Mondo"].records[0].concept_id
'mondo:0005233'
```

```
param str query_str

query, a string, to search for

param str incl

str containing comma-separated names of sources to
use. Will exclude all other sources. Case-insensitive.

param str excl

str containing comma-separated names of source to
exclude. Will include all other source. Case-insensitive.

return

dict containing all matches found in sources.

rtype

dict

raises InvalidParameterException

if both incl and excl args are
provided, or if invalid source names are given.
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._add_merged_meta
documentation:
Add source metadata to response object.

```
param Dict response

in-progress response object

return

completed response object.
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._add_disease
documentation:
Format received DB record as core Disease object and update response object.

```
param response

in-progress response object

param record

record as stored in DB

param match_type

type of match achieved

return

completed normalized response object ready to return to user
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._record_order
documentation:
Construct priority order for matching. Only called by sort().

```
param Dict record

individual record item in iterable to sort

return

tuple with rank value and concept ID
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler._handle_failed_merge_ref
documentation:
Log + fill out response for a failed merge reference lookup.

```
param Dict record

record containing failed merge_ref

param Dict response

in-progress response object

param str query

original query value

return

Normalized response with no match
```

methods.name: disease.query.QueryHandler.normalize
documentation:
Return normalized concept for query.

Use to retrieve normalized disease concept records:

```pycon
>>> from disease.query import QueryHandler
>>> from disease.database import create_db
>>> q = QueryHandler(create_db())
>>> result = q.normalize("NSCLC")
>>> result.normalized_id
'ncit:C2926'
```

```
param query

String to find normalized concept for

return

Normalized disease concept
```

Class for normalizer management. Stores reference to database instance
and normalizes query input.

modules.name: disease.database.postgresql
documentation: Provide PostgreSQL client.

classes.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.__init__
documentation:
Initialize Postgres connection.

```pycon
>>> from disease.database.postgresql import PostgresDatabase
>>> db = PostgresDatabase(
>>>     user="postgres",
>>>     password="matthew_cannon2",
>>>     db_name="disease_normalizer"
>>> )
```

```
param db_url

libpq compliant database connection URI

Keyword Arguments

user: Postgres username

password: Postgres password (optional or blank if unneeded)

db_name: name of database to connect to

raise DatabaseInitializationException

if initial setup fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.list_tables
documentation:
Return names of tables in database.

```
return

Table names in database
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.drop_db
documentation:
Perform complete teardown of DB. Useful for quickly resetting all data or
reconstructing after apparent schema error. If in a protected environment,
require confirmation.

:raise DatabaseWriteException: if called in a protected setting with
    confirmation silenced.

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.check_schema_initialized
documentation:
Check if database schema is properly initialized.

```
return

True if DB appears to be fully initialized, False otherwise
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.check_tables_populated
documentation:
Perform rudimentary checks to see if tables are populated.
Emphasis is on rudimentary -- if some fiendish element has deleted half of the
disease aliases, this method won't pick it up. It just wants to see if a few
critical tables have at least a small number of records.

```
return

True if queries successful, false if DB appears empty
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.initialize_db
documentation: Check if DB is set up. If not, create tables/indexes/views.

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase._create_views
documentation: Create materialized views.

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase._refresh_views
documentation:
Update materialized views.

Not responsible for ensuring existence of views. Calling functions should
either check beforehand or catch psycopg.UndefinedTable.

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase._drop_indexes

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase._create_tables

```
ID of concept to get
```

Retrieve normalized record from DB.


methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.get_record_by_id
documentation:
Fetch record corresponding to provided concept ID

```
param str concept_id

concept ID for disease record

param bool case_sensitive

not used by this implementation -- lookups use
case-insensitive index

param bool merge

if true, look for merged record; look for identity record
otherwise.

return

complete disease record, if match is found; None otherwise
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.get_refs_by_type
documentation:
Retrieve concept IDs for records matching the user's query. Other methods
are responsible for actually retrieving full records.

```
param search_term

string to match against

param ref_type

type of match to look for.

return

list of associated concept IDs. Empty if lookup fails.
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.get_all_concept_ids
documentation:
Retrieve concept IDs for use in generating normalized records.

```
param source

optionally, just get all IDs for a specific source

return

Set of concept IDs as strings.
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.get_all_records
documentation:
Retrieve all source or normalized records. Either return all source records,
or all records that qualify as "normalized" (i.e., merged groups + source
records that are otherwise ungrouped).

For example,

```pycon
>>> from disease.database import create_db
>>> from disease.schemas import RecordType
>>> db = create_db()
>>> for record in db.get_all_records(RecordType.MERGER):
>>>     pass  # do something
```

Unlike DynamoDB, merged records are stored in a separate table from source
records. As a result, when fetching all normalized records, merged records are
return first, and iteration continues with all source records that don't
belong to a normalized concept group.

```
param record_type

type of result to return

return

Generator that lazily provides records as they are retrieved
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.add_source_metadata
documentation:
Add new source metadata entry.

```
param src_name

name of source

param meta

known source attributes

raise DatabaseWriteException

if write fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.add_record
documentation:
Add new record to database.

```
param record

record to upload

param src_name

name of source for record. Not used by PostgreSQL instance.
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.add_merged_record
documentation:
Add merged record to database.

```
param record

merged record to add
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.update_merge_ref
documentation:
Update the merged record reference of an individual record to a new value.

```
param concept_id

record to update

param merge_ref

new ref value

raise DatabaseWriteException

if attempting to update non-existent record
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.delete_normalized_concepts
documentation:
Remove merged records from the database. Use when performing a new update
of normalized data.

It would be faster to drop the entire table and do a cascading delete onto the
merge_ref column in disease_concepts, but that requires an exclusive access lock
on the DB, which can be annoying (ie you couldn't have multiple processes
accessing it, or PgAdmin, etc...). Instead, we'll take down each merge table
dependency and rebuild afterwards.

:raise DatabaseReadException: if DB client requires separate read calls and
    encounters a failure in the process
:raise DatabaseWriteException: if deletion call fails

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.delete_source
documentation:
Delete all data for a source. Use when updating source data.

All of the foreign key relations make deletes *extremely* slow, so this method
drops and then re-adds them once deletes are finished. This makes it a little
brittle, and it'd be nice to revisit in the future to perform as a single
atomic transaction.

Refreshing the materialized view at the end might be redundant, because
this method will almost always be called right before more data is written,
but it's probably necessary just in case that doesn't happen.

```
param src_name

name of source to delete

raise DatabaseWriteException

if deletion call fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.complete_write_transaction
documentation: Conclude transaction or batch writing if relevant.

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.close_connection
documentation: Perform any manual connection closure procedures if necessary.

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.load_from_remote
documentation:
Load DB from remote dump. Warning: Deletes all existing data. If not
passed as an argument, will try to grab latest release from VICC S3 bucket.

```
param url

location of .tar.gz file created from output of pg_dump

raise DatabaseException

if unable to retrieve file from URL or if psql
command fails
```

methods.name: disease.database.postgresql.PostgresDatabase.export_db
documentation:
Dump DB to specified location.

```
param export_location

path to directory to save DB dump in

return

Nothing, but saves results of pg_dump to file named
disease_norm_<date and time>.sql

raise ValueError

if output directory isn't a directory or doesn't exist

raise DatabaseException

if psql call fails
```
Disease Normalizer database client for PostgreSQL.

modules.name: disease.cli
documentation: Provides a CLI util to make updates to normalizer database.

functions.name: disease.cli._configure_logging
documentation: Configure logging.

functions.name: disease.cli.check_db
documentation:
Perform basic checks on DB health and population. Exits with status code 1
if DB schema is uninitialized or if critical tables appear to be empty.

```
param db_url

URL to normalizer database

param verbose

if true, print result to console
```

functions.name: disease.cli.update_from_remote
documentation:
Update data from remotely-hosted DB dump. By default, fetches from latest
available dump on VICC S3 bucket; specific URLs can be provided instead by
command line option or DISEASE_NORM_REMOTE_DB_URL environment variable.

```
param data_url

user-specified location to pull DB dump from

param db_url

URL to normalizer database
```

functions.name: disease.cli.dump_database
documentation:
Dump data from database into file.

```
param output_directory

path to existing directory

param db_url

URL to normalizer database
```

```
records

param from_local
```


functions.name: disease.cli.update_db
documentation:
Update selected normalizer source(s) in the disease database.

```
param sources

names of sources to update, comma-separated

param aws_instance

if true, use cloud instance

param db_url
```
